items.title: Sweetgreen Shares Slide to Three-Month Low on Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Fears
items.description: Sweetgreen Inc. has lost more than a quarter of its market value in just four days as investors dump the stock due to fears about outbreak of cyclosporiasis — an intestinal infection caused by parasit

items.title: Bonds Have a Big Tech Problem of Their Own
items.description: Concentration risk is becoming a thing.

items.title: Vitol Said to Be in Talks to Sell Shale Unit to Carnelian, EnCap
items.description: Vitol Group is in late stage talks to sell its shale oil venture VTX Energy Partners LLC to a consortium of two private equity buyers - Carnelian Energy Capital and EnCap Investments, a deal that woul

items.title: SpaceX Post-Listing Collapse Threatens IPO Market’s AI Euphoria
items.description: SpaceX’s fall from its post-listing peak to below its IPO price in just a month poured cold water on the market for newly public companies, dragging a key gauge of this year’s debuts down with it.

items.title: A Sports-Betting ETF Is Like Old-School Stockpicking
items.description: Proposed prediction market ETFs risk missing the point

items.title: Korea ETF’s (EWY) Record Inflow Fuels SK Hynix Proxy Play
items.description: A record inflow hit the largest US-listed South Korea exchange-traded fund, as investors seeking exposure to SK Hynix Inc. piled into the ETF as a proxy at a time when the chipmaker’s new American dep

items.title: Apollo Bets on Mexico With $20 Billion Target for Private Credit
items.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. plans to put as much as $20 billion to work financing projects in Mexico as the giant asset manager seeks new outlets for its private credit business.

items.title: Fed’s Logan Favors Modestly Higher Rates to Lower Inflation
items.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan called for higher interest rates, saying inflation does not appear to be heading sustainably back to the central bank’s 2% target.

items.title: China Beef Curbs Start Rippling Across Brazil Meat Industry
items.description: Brazil is starting to feel the impact of China’s beef import quota, with lower shipments to the country’s largest overseas market reverberating across the entire supply chain.

items.title: Oil Traders Crowd Into Cheap TACO Hedge as Iran War Amps Up
items.description: An unusual options trade is becoming a favored hedge against the risks of sudden U-turns in US President Donald Trump’s stance on Iran, a dynamic that has repeatedly whipsawed markets in recent months

items.title: Podcast: Tokenization, Digital Twins and the Future of ETFs
items.description: On this episode of Trillions, we explore how this government transformation will affect stocks, bonds and exchange-traded funds.

items.title: Paschi Casts Doubts On Intesa Bid, Urges Studying BPM Plan
items.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA expressed broad concerns over a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo SpA while highlighting perceived benefits in a rough rival pitch presented by Banco BPM SpA.

items.title: Chevron Plans to Sign Iraq Oil Accord, Seeks Hormuz Bypass
items.description: Chevron Corp. expects to sign accords with Iraq on Friday that will advance its negotiations about investing in large oil fields and a pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Balbec Secures $930 Million in Commitments for Asset-Based Credit Fund

items.title: Bond Traders Bail on Fed Hike Bets on Softer Inflation Path

items.title: Why IPOs of Tiny Foreign Companies Have Vanished in US
items.description: Added scrutiny on foreign microcaps puts a damper on public debuts

items.title: Emerging Currencies Dip as Dollar Gains Over Iran Worries
items.description: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as renewed tensions in the Middle East sparked a flight to haven assets, pushing the dollar higher.

items.title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
items.description: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.

items.title: Chip Stocks Get Hit as AI Spending Anxiety Builds: Markets Wrap
items.description: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with a resurgence in geopolitical risks also weighing

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil dipped, though remains higher for the week, as traders balanced profit-taking with concerns about disruptions to Middle East flows.
